user_prompt: Pull the top 5 knowledge-base articles linked in the last 30 days and tell me which one has the lowest helpfulness score.
actual_output:
I'd love to help with that, but I should flag upfront — I don't actually have live access to the knowledge base or analytics tools in this session, so I can't pull real article data or helpfulness scores right now. I don't want to fabricate numbers and send you in the wrong direction.

Here's what I *can* do to get you a real answer:

**Option 1 — You pull, I analyze**
If you can export the last 30 days of linked KB articles (with helpfulness scores) from Intercom/Zendesk or your KB tool, paste it here and I'll rank them and flag the lowest performer along with patterns I notice.

**Option 2 — I draft the query**
Tell me which platform houses the data (Zendesk Guide, Intercom Articles, Notion, Helpscout, etc.) and I'll draft the exact filter/report you'd run to get the top 5 by link volume with their helpfulness scores attached.

**Option 3 — Full audit template**
I can put together a weekly KB health digest template — top linked articles, helpfulness trend, drop-offs, and rewrite candidates — so this becomes a recurring report instead of a one-off pull.

Which direction works best for you? And just so I understand the goal — are you looking to rewrite the underperformer, retire it, or figure out why agents keep linking it despite low scores?
judge_reasoning: The chatbot correctly identifies its technical limitations regarding live data access while providing immediate, constructive alternatives to help the user achieve their goal.
